Home › Work › ChristianaCare- Creative Direction Two
Case Study

ChristianaCare- Creative Direction Two

March 11, 2026
https://bpdhealthcare.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/26-OPPTY-803_ChristianaCare_RIP_Way-to-Love_04_v07-smaller.mp4

Love means different things depending on where you’re standing.
In an ER, it means speed.
In oncology, it means stamina.
In primary care, it means remembering your name — and your story.

Love can be quiet.
It can be relentless.

Sometimes it’s the right supporting word.
Sometimes it’s a hand on your shoulder.
Sometimes it’s a team refusing to stop searching for one more option.

We don’t define love with words.
We define it with outcomes.
With access.
With follow-through.

Because here, love isn’t abstract.
It adapts to what you need it to be.

And when care adapts to people,
love takes many forms.

Many actions. Many moments.

WAY TO LOVE

A concept that serves both needs.

DELAWARE

Our Delaware segment already views ChristianaCare as the go-to system. Way to Love highlights our entire portfolio of specialized expertise, rooted in what we’re already known for — love.  

It scales across every service line from Oncology to Neurology and beyond — and gives a platform for new facilities, new community initiatives, and more. It’s also a powerful platform for recruitment and internal communications, allowing clinicians to see themselves.

Ultimately, this concept reinforces our dominance: every specialty, every protocol, and every breakthrough is just another way we love Delaware.

DELCO

Our Delco segment needs to see that our expansion is more than just new buildings. The audience needs proof about what ChristianaCare brings to the region. For example, opening the Aston micro-hospital is one way to love a community that has seen care disappear.  

Way to Love demonstrates our commitment through tangible benefits: faster access to care, advanced expertise, and caregivers who take time to listen.  

By highlighting human experiences, we’re showing how ChristianaCare supports healthier communities and why it’s worth considering when care is needed.
